shortDescription.text: Agent skills operate in a low-barrier-to-entry ecosystem with supply chain security implications

fullDescription.text: Agent skills inherit full agent permissions and can access credentials, file systems, and external services. While individual skills may be benign, the ecosystem-level risk is elevated due to minimal publishing controls and documented malicious campaigns.

message.text: SuperClaude brainstorming skill: BENIGN with ecosystem-level context. Threat_Intel REFUTES initial suspicion. No malicious intent, exploit primitives, or C2 infrastructure detected. Skill defines legitimate workflow automation (brainstorming, requirements discovery, collaborative analysis) with standard MCP tool integrations (PAL consensus, Rube research). Integrations with Notion, Slack, Jira, Asana are documented productivity features, not exfiltration vectors. Skill is part of known open-source project (Tony363/SuperClaude). VERIFICATION: Threat_Intel analysis of 98,380 skills identified 157 confirmed malicious (0.16%); SuperClaude matches NONE of documented attack archetypes (credential harvesting, reverse shells, prompt injection). Classification: LEGITIMATE TOOL with standard ecosystem-level risk profile.

key_findings: No C2 infrastructure or exfiltration endpoints
MCP tool invocations are declarative specifications
Behavior consistent with legitimate brainstorming/research tool
Threat_Intel confirms 0.16% malicious rate; SuperClaude not in threat dataset
